title: End-of-Life Wishes
description: Document how you'd like to be remembered and cared for. This conversation helps your family honor your wishes with clarity and confidence.

questions.text: How would you like your body to be cared for after you pass?
questions.sentenceTemplate: For my body, I would like [blank].

questions.text: What would you like done with your ashes?
questions.sentenceTemplate: I would like my ashes to be [blank].

questions.text: Where would you like to be buried?
questions.sentenceTemplate: I would like to be buried [blank].

questions.text: What kind of service or ceremony would you prefer?
questions.sentenceTemplate: For my service, I would prefer [blank].

questions.text: Is there a specific song, reading, or tradition you'd like included?
questions.sentenceTemplate: I would like my service to include [blank].

questions.text: Who should be notified first when you pass?

title: Medical Emergency Planning
description: Documenting your healthcare preferences and emergency details. This isn't just about paperwork — it's about peace of mind for you and your loved ones.

questions.text: Who should be the first call in a medical emergency?
questions.sentenceTemplate: In a medical emergency, please call [blank] first.

questions.text: What are your thoughts on life-sustaining treatments (DNR)?
questions.sentenceTemplate: For life-sustaining treatment, my preference is [blank].

questions.text: Where do you keep your physical health records?
Do you have any known allergies or important medical conditions to note?
Who do you authorize to make medical decisions on your behalf?
questions.sentenceTemplate: I authorize [blank] to make medical decisions for me.

title: Finances & Estate
description: A thoughtful walkthrough of your financial picture and estate plans. Helps your family avoid confusion and unnecessary stress during difficult times.

questions.text: Do you have a will or trust in place?
Where is your will or trust document located?
Who is your primary financial beneficiary?
Do you have life insurance? If so, where is that policy?
Who should handle your financial affairs if you cannot?
questions.sentenceTemplate: I would like [blank] to handle my financial affairs.
